name: MCC East Bay
mission: Join us in making a difference by donating today and helping us continue our vital work in serving the community.

evaluationSources: Special-Needs Friendly Masjid Certification (MUHSEN (Muslims Understanding and Helping Special Education Needs))
Gold-Certified Masjid (MUHSEN (Muslims Understanding and Helping Special Education Needs))
Muslim Appreciation and Awareness Month Proclamation (Pleasanton City Council)
Muslim Appreciation and Awareness Month Proclamation (Livermore City Council)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mcceastbay.org/zakat (Source: https://mcceastbay.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: ibn_sabil (+5); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 30/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish current reserve levels and a board-approved reserve policy (often 3-12 months for most nonprofits).
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

components.evidence: Theory of change: BASIC (emerging org baseline)
components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

components.evidence: Board governance: baseline (2 members, emerging org)
components.name: Governance

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Board too small: 2 members

score_details.score_summary: Muslim Community Center - East Bay shows good alignment and limited impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://mcceastbay.org/zakat (Source: https://mcceastbay.org/zakat)

all_citations.claim: The organization focuses on the emergence of an American Muslim identity and provides direct services like a food pantry.
The charity maintains a Zakat-eligible fund and follows specific asnaf categories for distribution.
The organization offers various donation options and programs including a car donation program.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its high alignment with donor values and direct service model, though its total impact score is constrained by limited data transparency.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public availability of detailed impact metrics and cost-per-beneficiary data [1]
Low data confidence scores suggest a need for increased financial transparency and third-party verification [1]

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is high for Muslim donors due to the organization's focus on religious identity and its strict adherence to Zakat eligibility requirements for charitable distributions [2][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization's impact is characterized by direct service delivery, such as its food pantry and Zakat distribution, though a lack of granular financial data and third-party verification results in a lower impact score [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Community Center – East Bay serves as a vital hub for the emergence of an American Muslim identity through comprehensive social services and religious programming.
strengths: Maintains a Zakat-eligible fund that adheres to traditional asnaf categories for distribution [2]
Provides direct community services including a food pantry and vehicle donation program [1]
Offers multiple giving channels to facilitate community support and religious obligations [4]
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Center – East Bay (MCC East Bay) is a religious organization dedicated to fostering a distinct American Muslim identity while providing essential community support [1]. The center operates diverse humanitarian programs, including a food pantry, a car donation program, and a dedicated Zakat fund to assist those in need [2][4].
